surface.name: HTTP predictions API via cURL (API token + prepaid credit)
surface.selection_basis: Replicate's Create a prediction guide documents cURL examples with Prefer: wait against https://api.replicate.com/v1/predictions and Authorization: Bearer $REPLICATE_API_TOKEN. Prepaid credit docs state you must first purchase credit upfront before usage. An SDK install is not required.

alternatives_considered.name: Node.js get-started scaffold
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Local Node.js package and project setup; the HTTP API documents the same predictions call with cURL.

alternatives_considered.name: Python get-started
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Peer SDK route; not required when official HTTP docs show cURL.

alternatives_considered.name: Google Colab get-started
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Notebook path with additional Google Colab prerequisites.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Replicate account, purchase prepaid credit, create an API token, and receive a successful prediction JSON result over HTTPS.
assumed_prior_state: No Replicate account, API token, prepaid credit, or prediction is assumed.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.replicate.com/v1/predictions with Prefer: wait returns a prediction whose status is successful and whose output field is populated.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The prediction JSON includes status successful (or equivalent terminal success) and a non-null output for the requested model input.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Create a prediction documents Prefer: wait cURL to /v1/predictions and shows a response with output populated and status successful for replicate/hello-world.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful hosted model success is a completed prediction with output. cURL is the documented HTTP path. Prepaid credit must be purchased upfront per billing docs before usage can proceed.

prerequisites.requirement: A Replicate account that can open billing settings and API tokens.
Prepaid credit purchased with a payment method. Prepaid credit docs state you must first purchase credit upfront; usage deducts from the balance.
An API token from the account API tokens page, used as Authorization: Bearer.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to a Replicate account.
details: API tokens and billing require an authenticated session (GitHub sign-in is documented on the tokens page).
primary_path.input: Intent to run models on Replicate
primary_path.success_signal: Billing settings and API tokens are reachable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account recovery follows the sign-in flow.

primary_path.action: Add a payment method and purchase prepaid credit.
details: Prepaid credit docs: add a payment method in billing settings, then Add credit.
Purchased credit is valid for 1 year and is not refundable.
When the balance hits zero, new work is prevented.
primary_path.success_signal: Billing shows a usable credit balance.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without prepaid credit, predictions cannot start under the prepaid model.

primary_path.action: Create or copy an API token and store it securely.
details: Export as REPLICATE_API_TOKEN for the documented cURL Authorization header.
primary_path.success_signal: The token value is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for token creation.

primary_path.output: Token available to HTTP clients in the shell
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: POST a sync prediction with cURL to https://api.replicate.com/v1/predictions.
details: Include Prefer: wait, Authorization: Bearer $REPLICATE_API_TOKEN, and Content-Type: application/json.
Send version and input as in the Create a prediction cURL example (for example replicate/hello-world).
primary_path.success_signal: The response includes a prediction object with populated output.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Zero credit, invalid token, or long-running models may return incomplete status requiring poll via urls.get.

primary_path.action: Confirm the prediction JSON shows successful status and non-null output.
primary_path.success_signal: output is populated and status is successful.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: error status or null output means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers an official client SDK.
branches.path: Use the JavaScript or Python client to create the same prediction; token and prepaid credit requirements stay the same.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds optional package installation without changing the prediction boundary.

branches.condition: Sync wait times out before completion.
branches.path: Poll GET urls.get until status is successful or failed.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds polling wait without changing credentials or billing gates.

friction_gates.description: Prepaid credit must be purchased with a payment method before usage under the prepaid model.
A Bearer API token is required on every predictions request.

sources.title: Prepaid credit
sections_used: What happens when you run out of credit

sources.title: Billing
evidence_supported: Select models free briefly then billing setup

uncertainties.question: Whether any remaining free-limit or legacy arrears accounts can complete a first prediction without prepaid purchase.
uncertainties.impact: Billing step ordering could differ for rare account states.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Billing still mentions free limits and arrears accounts while prepaid credit says purchase is required to use Replicate.
